title: origanum × sevcaniae
intro: Origanum × sevcaniae, also known as Turkish oregano, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Origanum minutiflorum and Origanum scutatum, and it belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved digestion. The essential oil extracted from Turkish oregano has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and topical creams, to treat various health issues, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Turkish oregano also include its potential to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
